hypothesis: When evaluated under a matched numerical integration scheme (identical solver and 500 function evaluations), flow-matching 3D molecular generative models will achieve a mean GFN2-xTB relaxation energy (∆Erelax) within 10% of diffusion-based models on the corrected GEOM-Drugs benchmark, indicating that the previously observed performance gap is primarily due to discretization rather than an intrinsic modeling advantage.
